title: Section 23D
content: If any person, who is registered under section 12 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India Act, 1992 (15 of 1992) as a stock broker or sub-broker, fails to segregate securities or moneys of the client or clients or uses the securities or moneys of a client or clients for self or for any other client, he shall be 1[liable to a penalty which shall not be less than one lakh rupees but which may extend to one crore rupees].
footnote: 1. Subs. by s. 28, ibid., for "liable to a penalty not exceeding one crore rupees" (w.e.f. 8-9-2014).
